manifesto: I code things that didn't exist yesterday.
If you're looking for corporate, that's not me.

items.tag: Apps that work. Not slides that talk.
items.desc: Custom web and mobile applications, business process automation, API integrations. Clean, shippable code.

items.tag: No buzzwords. Agents that do the job.
items.desc: Model integration, autonomous agents, data processing, RAG, fine-tuning. What makes sense, not what looks pretty.

items.tag: Models, renders, worlds. From pixel to polygon.
items.desc: Modeling, rigging, rendering, three.js / WebGL integration, product visualization. The avatar above — I made it.

items.tag: I debug your team before your code.
items.desc: Coaching on AI, digital, tech project management. For teams in doubt, or those moving too fast.

about.name: JOHANN KAISER.
about.p1: 14 years independent. Dev, AI, 3D, training. Based in Paris 12<sup>th</sup>. I work remote, on-site in Île-de-France when it's useful, and I've been refusing open offices since 2012.

contact.label: Write me.
contact.note: Reply within 24h. Most of the time.

items.question: Who are you?
answers: Johann Kaiser. Independent since 2012. I code, I model, I advise. Not an agency, not a team — just me.
OK, let me say it again. Johann Kaiser, freelance for fourteen years. I do dev, 3D, and consulting. I work alone, no team.
You know what, I'm starting to think you're not really listening. Same answer. Try a real question.

items.question: What do you do in dev?
answers: Custom web and mobile applications. No WordPress, no recycled Shopify templates. Code I sign off on.
Saying it differently: I build web and mobile apps, by hand, custom. Nothing from a template factory.
Look, at this point I'd suggest taking a listening class. Custom dev, made-to-measure, period.

items.question: And AI?
answers: I integrate agents and models into tools that actually save time. No buzzwords, no gadget chatbots. Stuff that works.
If you didn't catch it: I use AI to automate concrete tasks. Not to look fancy on a slide.
You're persistent, huh. Fine — AI, for real. There. Next?

items.question: Do you do 3D too?
answers: Yes. Modeling, rendering, product visualization, assets for digital or print. The avatar you're looking at — I made it.
Saying it again: yes, I do 3D. And yes, once again, that's me you're watching speak. Hi.
Look, at some point either you take my word for it, or you watch me do it eyes-to-eyes. Right now you're doing both.

items.question: And consulting?
answers: I work with teams on AI, digital, tech project management. Often I debug the human before I touch the code.
OK, consulting. I come in, I listen, I say what I see. And sometimes I write code. Mostly I pull people out of the fog.
What exactly are you trying to confirm? Consulting is consulting. We're not inventing a new word.

items.question: Why you rather than an agency?
answers: Because you're talking to the person who codes. Not a salesperson who'll later pass you to a junior. Fast decisions, direct execution.
Shorter version: you want custom work, not a seven-step process? Don't go to an agency.
If at this point you still don't have the answer, I don't know — go to an agency, come back in six months.

items.question: How much does it cost?
answers: Depends on the project. Flat rate for scoped missions, daily rate for consulting. We talk, I quote you clearly, no surprises.
OK, the price. It depends, really. Give me the project, I give you a number. No opaque grid.
You think I'll throw a random number without knowing the project? Come on, let's move.

items.question: Do you take long projects?
answers: Yes, but not full-time. I work on several missions in parallel, that's what keeps me sharp. We set the rhythm at the start.
Saying it again: yes to long projects, no to full-time at one place. Multiple things in parallel — that's my rhythm.
Long, short, medium. At this rate you're going to ask if I take the metro. I've already answered twice, I'm not doing it again.

items.question: What tech do you use?
answers: JavaScript, Python, Three.js, the Claude and OpenAI APIs, Blender for 3D. Right tool for the right job, not the other way around.
OK, the stack. JS, Python, Three.js, the AI APIs, Blender. And whatever else fits the project.
You want a technical CV in clean columns? I can email it to you. Next.

items.question: Do you work remotely?
answers: Yes, by default. I'm based in Paris, I can come on-site in the Paris region if it's useful. The rest, we do over video.
Remote, yes. On-site when it's worth it, in Paris or the Île-de-France. No relocation needed.
At this point I'm starting to wonder if you're testing my patience. Remote. There. Really.

items.question: Are there things you refuse?
answers: Projects without a clear vision, people who just want to tick an AI box, and anything that requires lying to users. The rest, we talk.
Shorter version: no bullshit, no AI greenwashing, no manipulation. The rest, open.
Are you trying to find out if I'll refuse to answer your next question? Bingo, you're getting there.

items.question: How do I start with you?
answers: You email me at johann dot metagora at gmail dot com. We schedule a call, see if we're aligned, and we start. Simple.
Not more complicated than that: an email, a call, and if we click we kick off. My email's right at the bottom of the page.
Seriously? At this point, just email me for real, because we've gone around three times already.
